mnemonics.title: STATIC Memory
mnemonics.content:
S - Single copy for class
T - Type defined outside class
A - Accessible via class name
T - Total objects share it
I - Initialized once
C - Can count objects created

acronyms.usage: Remember static variables have static storage duration, function scope, and are shared across all objects

visualTips.visualization: Draw a class box with two sections: one showing static members outside individual objects but connected to all, and instance members inside each object separately. Use a cloud to represent shared/static area.
Show the class declaration as a blueprint, then show the separate definition outside as allocating actual memory in the data segment - like placing one item in a shared warehouse.

analogies.analogy: Like a communal refrigerator in an office - one refrigerator shared by all employees (objects), not one per employee.
Like a company phone directory - you can access it without needing to visit a specific employee's desk (object), just use the company (class) name.
